intro: Porcius Festus was the Roman procurator of Judea who succeeded Felix (Acts 24:27), appointed approximately 59–60 A.D. He inherited the case of Paul, who had been imprisoned for two years under Felix. When the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem pressed for Paul's transfer to stand trial there, Festus offered a hearing in Jerusalem instead, leading Paul to exercise his right as a Roman citizen and appeal to Caesar (Acts 25:11–12). Festus arranged a second hearing before King Agrippa II and Bernice, at which Paul gave his great defense speech (Acts 26). Festus's comment — "Paul, you are out of your mind; your great learning is driving you mad" (Acts 26:24) — captures his inability to comprehend Paul's argument about resurrection and prophecy. Festus died in office after a short governorship.
